hero_subtitle: A historic and spiritual sacred place in Jamalpur
about_text: Doyamoyee Temple, located at Zero Point in Jamalpur, is a historic Hindu temple established in 1698 (1104 Bengali Era) during the reign of Nawab Murshid Quli Khan. Founded by Jagirdar Sri Krishna Roy Chowdhury, the temple complex includes shrines dedicated to Shiva, Kali, Nat Mandir, and Manasa Devi. It is renowned for its centuries-old murals and traditional architectural heritage.
history_text: For over 328 years, the temple has remained a center of daily worship and spiritual devotion. During the annual Ashtami Fair, devotees from across the country gather in large numbers. The Debottor Estate was established by Rani Shrimati Narayani Roy Chowdhury, and the temple’s structural development was later enhanced by Raja Jogendra Kishore Roy Chowdhury. The temple remains open daily from 6:00 AM to 10:00 PM.
founder: Founder: Jagirdar Sri Krishna Roy Chowdhury
estate_founder: Debottor Estate Founder: Rani Shrimati Narayani Roy Chowdhury
development: Infrastructure Development: Raja Jogendra Kishore Roy Chowdhury

temple.name: History of Shri Shri Ri Doyamoyee Temple
temple.history: One of the most prominent religious institutions for Sanatan Dharm followers in the subcontinent, Shri Shri Ri Doyamoyee Temple was established in Bengali 1104 by Shreekrishna Roy Chowdhury, a close associate of Murshidkuli Khan, the zamindar of Zafarshahi and Mymensingh. His daughter-in-law Narayani Chowdhurani, during the time of Bengal Governor General Warren Hastings, established the 'Shri Shri Ri Doyamoyee Debottor Estate' for temple expenditure management. Their descendants, zamindar Jogendra Kishore Roy Chowdhury of Ramgopalpur, undertook extensive development work of the temple.
temple.deities: In Shri Shri Ri Doyamoyee Temple, along with the idol of Shri Shri Ri Doyamoyee Mahadebya Mata, there are also idols of Sri Sri ŋree Annapurna Mata and Sri Sri Shiva. Before independence, the idol of Sri Sri ŋree Dashabhuja Mata was also established in the temple.
temple.rituals: Daily worship includes jagaran, bhog, bali, yajna, and evening aarti. Special pujas are conducted on every Amavasya for Shri Shri Ri Doyamoyee Mahadebya Mata, on Purnima for Sri Sri ŋree Annapurna Mata, and on Chaturdashi for Sri Sri Shiva. Seasonal vegetables produced in accordance with nature are offered to the deities. Offerings include one-month honey and sweet bhog from 1st Boishakh, date palm pitha on 13th Bhadra, two-month Balyabhog from 8th Poush to 8th Falgun, Pitha-Payesh on Poush Sankranti, and one-month Sheetala Mata bhog in Magh. Before independence (1971), daily goat sacrifices were performed and 30 devotees received prasadam.
temple.annual_festivals: Every year, during Sharadiya Sri Sri Durga Puja and the anniversary of the temple on Sri Sri Dipanbita Kali Puja, various programs are organized with grandeur, including worship and other activities. Prasadam is distributed to all families in the city. During Durga Puja, devotees offer Anjali, during Kali Puja lamps are lit, and on Bengali New Year, families with children visit the temple.
temple.special_rituals: In Shravana month, every Monday, numerous devotees take a holy dip in the Brahmaputra river and pour water over the head of Sri Sri Shiva idol at the temple. During Chaitra, at Sri Sri Basanti Puja, the temple becomes lively with devotees taking Ashtami baths in the river. Every year, devotees offer over two hundred goats as a vow to Shree ŋree Dayamoyi Mata. Devotees from home and abroad come daily to fulfill their vows and leave the temple satisfied.
temple.management_history: Before independence, Shri Shri Ri Doyamoyee Temple was managed through the 'Shri Shri Ri Doyamoyee Estate' and its appointed staff. During the Liberation War of 1971, the temple and the idol of Shri Shri Ri Doyamoyee Mahadebya Mata suffered significant destruction. In 1972, the Government of Bangladesh took over the 'Shri Shri Ri Doyamoyee Estate' without compensation. Post-independence, in 1976, devotees of Jamalpur formed the Shri Shri Ri Doyamoyee Temple Management Committee, which continues to manage the temple through donations.
temple.governance: In Bengali 1403, the Temple Management Committee established a constitution. Annual General Meetings are held every Chaitra month, and every 2 years, a 31-member Executive Committee is elected by members of the Management Committee. The Committee has 18 life members, 352 general members, and 35 members of the Advisory Council. Currently, the temple staff consists of 12 people, including two priests. Annual accounts are audited and presented for approval. As daily expenses are not fully covered by donations, a two-storey building with 27 rooms has been constructed for rental income.
temple.development: After Bengali 1403, extensive development work has been carried out in the temple with support from local and international devotees, the honorable MP, and Jamalpur District Council. Construction includes the aesthetic Shri Shri Ri Doyamoyee idol temple, Sri Sri Shiva temple, NatTemple, and Sri Sri ŋree Annapurna temple, along with renovation work.
temple.national_events: On various national days, prayers are offered in the temple for the welfare of the country and world peace.
temple.library_scholarship: The temple has a well-stocked library. Every year, the temple provides the 'Shri Shri Ri Doyamoyee Educational Scholarship' to poor and meritorious female students and distributes clothing to the needy.

newImages.title: New Images
newImages.description: The new images of Shree Shree Ri Doyamoyee Temple offer devotees and visitors a fresh experience. These images showcase recent renovations, mandap, main gate, and modern architectural beauty of the temple. The new visuals reflect the spiritual charm and grandeur of Shree Shree Ri Doyamoyee Temple.

oldImages.title: Old Images
oldImages.description: The old images of Shree Shree Ri Doyamoyee Temple capture its heritage, ancient architecture, and memorable moments of devotees. These images preserve the history and timeless beauty of Shree Shree Ri Doyamoyee Temple, allowing visitors to experience its spiritual legacy.
